title: Collections, Taxonomy, Ordering, Navigation, Feeds, and Sitemaps · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for collections, taxonomy, ordering, navigation, feeds, and sitemaps and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, filesystem, parser, template, validator, host, CDN, DNS, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for reconciled accessible site graph and derived publications. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Select published documents by typed metadata, language, route, taxonomy, and time under deterministic query and ordering rules.
Normalize tag and category identity separately from labels, aliases, hierarchy, route, and language.
Build global, local, breadcrumb, previous-next, related, and multiple-way navigation from an acyclic visible route graph.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Collection Query · changed case · Quiz Collection Query
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz publishing team handles case 5890 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; test empty, malformed, deeply nested, Unicode, collision, unsafe URL, missing asset, stale cache, rejected, and recovery cases; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Select published documents by typed metadata, language, route, taxonomy, and time under deterministic query and ordering rules.

content.text: A quiz publishing team handles case 4553 during an accessibility-led community newsroom release. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Feed Sitemap
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Feed Sitemap: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz publishing team handles case 8696 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile navigation, taxonomy, feeds, and sitemaps to one published route manifest; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only.
